name: journey
description: Native user-journey chart — sections of tasks, each tagged with actor(s) and a 1-5 mood. Renders as section bars, task chips, plumb lines, and mood faces.
purpose: Use when a process or experience needs charting as a horizontal sequence of moments, each scored for affect. Five variants reshape the same source list: default (Mermaid-style classic), heatmap (mood-tinted chips), curve (mood polyline with axis), swimlane (per-actor rows), weighted (chip widths proportional to `+volume`).

heading.description: Slide heading naming the journey or process.

sections.description: Top-level li per section. Lead with the section name; nested ul carries tasks. Each task carries inline-code tokens: `@actor` (one or more), `:N` mood 1-5, optional `+N` volume (used by .weighted).

skeleton:
<!-- _class: journey -->

## Walking through my Tuesday morning.

- Wake up
  - Hit snooze `@me` `:2`
  - Make coffee `@me` `:4`
- Commute
  - Subway `@me` `:1`
  - Walk `@me` `:5`
- Work
  - Standup `@team` `:3`
  - Deep work `@me` `:5`
sample:
<!-- _class: journey -->

## Customer onboarding · trial to activation.

- Evaluate
  - Read case study `@prospect` `:5`
  - Book demo `@prospect` `:4`
  - Live demo `@prospect` `@sales` `:4`
- Trial
  - Trial signup `@prospect` `:3`
  - Workspace setup `@user` `@onboarding` `:1`
- Activate
  - First report `@user` `:3`
  - Daily use `@user` `:5`

whenToUse.title: Affect is part of the story
whenToUse.body: When a process matters not just for its steps but for how each step feels. The 1-5 mood score makes the emotional contour part of the chart instead of buried in narration.

whenToUse.title: Actors share the trail
whenToUse.body: Use when multiple actors hand off through the sequence — customer, sales, onboarding, support. The `@actor` tokens make the handoff visible on every task chip.

whenToUse.title: One source, five lenses
whenToUse.body: Author the journey once and re-render under any variant. Heatmap for fastest scan, curve for trend, swimlane for actor load, weighted for traffic-mix — same data, different argument.

antiPatterns.title: Process without affect
antiPatterns.body: If the mood scores are all the same or arbitrary, the chart is doing less work than `timeline` or `list-steps`. Reserve journey for sequences where the affect changes meaningfully.

antiPatterns.title: More than ten tasks
antiPatterns.body: Past ten tasks the chips compress and the labels become unreadable. Group into fewer sections, or split the journey at a natural break.

antiPatterns.title: Volume tokens without weighted
antiPatterns.body: The `+N` volume token is meaningful only under the `weighted` variant. On the other four it is parsed but invisible — strip it from the markdown or commit to weighted.

related.name: list-steps
related.when: process needs descriptive body per step, no chart

related.name: gantt
related.when: schedule of overlapping tasks across lanes

related.name: kanban
related.when: current status by stage rather than sequence over time

heatmap.label: Heatmap — mood-tinted chips
heatmap.caption: Task chips are tinted by their mood score; plumb lines and faces are suppressed. The fastest variant to scan when the audience only needs to see which moments hurt.
heatmap.sample:
<!-- _class: journey heatmap -->

## Heatmap · where the trial drops off.

- Evaluate
  - Read case study `@prospect` `:5`
  - Book demo `@prospect` `:4`
- Trial
  - Trial signup `@prospect` `:3`
  - Workspace setup `@user` `:1`
- Activate
  - First report `@user` `:3`
  - Daily use `@user` `:5`

curve.label: Curve — mood polyline with axis
curve.caption: Renders the mood scores as a polyline over the task sequence with a mood gridline scale and section bands behind. Use when the trend across the journey is the headline.
curve.sample:
<!-- _class: journey curve -->

## Curve · the affect contour.

- Evaluate
  - Read case study `@prospect` `:5`
  - Book demo `@prospect` `:4`
- Trial
  - Trial signup `@prospect` `:3`
  - Workspace setup `@user` `:1`
- Activate
  - First report `@user` `:3`
  - Daily use `@user` `:5`

swimlane.label: Swimlane — per-actor rows
swimlane.caption: One row per actor; a dot marks every task that actor participates in, coloured by that task's mood. Use when the question is who carries the journey and where the handoffs land.
swimlane.sample:
<!-- _class: journey swimlane -->

## Swimlane · who owns each moment.

- Evaluate
  - Read case study `@prospect` `:5`
  - Live demo `@prospect` `@sales` `:4`
- Trial
  - Trial signup `@prospect` `:3`
  - Workspace setup `@user` `@onboarding` `:1`
- Activate
  - First report `@user` `:3`
  - Daily use `@user` `:5`

weighted.label: Weighted — chip widths by volume
weighted.caption: Chip widths scale to the `+volume` token; chip colour still encodes mood. Two dimensions at once — schedule shape and traffic-mix — for journeys with skewed loads.
weighted.sample:
<!-- _class: journey weighted -->

## Weighted · where the traffic actually lands.

- Discover
  - Search `@prospect` `:4` `+45`
  - Referral `@prospect` `:5` `+18`
- Convert
  - Pricing page `@prospect` `:3` `+12`
  - Checkout `@prospect` `:2` `+10`
- Support
  - Settings `@user` `:3` `+8`
  - Help docs `@user` `:4` `+7`
